records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.700 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Whoever sweeps the mosque on Thursday and the night of Jumu’ah (Thursday night) and removes from it even the dust that could irritate the eye, Allah (swt), the Exalted, will forgive them."
records.arabicText: 700 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «مَنْ كَنَسَ اَلْمَسْجِدَ يَوْمَ اَلْخَمِيسِ وَ لَيْلَةَ اَلْجُمُعَةِ فَأَخْرَجَ مِنْهُ مِنَ اَلتُّرَابِ مَا يُذَرُّ فِي اَلْعَيْنِ غَفَرَ اَللَّهُ تَعَالَى لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.700 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever sweeps the mosque on Thursday and the night of Jumu’ah (Thursday night) and removes from it even the dust that could irritate the eye, Allah (swt), the Exalted, will forgive them."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.701 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Whoever walks to the mosque, every step they take—whether it falls upon something moist or dry—glorifies Allah (swt) for them down to the seventh earth”.
records.arabicText: 701 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ مَشَى إِلَى اَلْمَسْجِدِ لَمْ يَضَعْ رِجْلَيْهِ عَلَى رَطْبٍ وَ لاَ يَابِسٍ إِلاَّ يُسَبِّحُ لَهُ إِلَى اَلْأَرَضِينَ اَلسَّابِعَةِ. وقد أخرجت هذه الأخبار مسندة وما رويت في معناها في كتاب فضل المساجد وحرمتها وما جاء فيها.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.701 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever walks to the mosque, every step they take—whether it falls upon something moist or dry—glorifies Allah (swt) for them down to the seventh earth”.

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.702 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said :
"A prayer in Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) is equivalent to one thousand prayers. A prayer in the Great Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) is equivalent to one hundred thousand prayers. A prayer in the Mosque of the Tribe (Masjid al-Qabilah) is equivalent to twenty-five prayers. A prayer in the market mosque is equivalent to twelve prayers. A man's prayer in his home is equivalent to one prayer."
records.arabicText: 702 - وَقَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «صَلاَةٌ فِي بَيْتِ اَلْمَقْدِسِ تَعْدِلُ أَلْفَ صَلاَةٍ وَ صَلاَةٌ فِي اَلْمَسْجِدِ اَلْأَعْظَمِ تَعْدِلُ مِائَةَ أَلْفِ صَلاَةٍ وَ صَلاَةٌ فِي مَسْجِدِ اَلْقَبِيلَةِ تَعْدِلُ خَمْساً وَ عِشْرِينَ صَلاَةً وَ صَلاَةٌ فِي مَسْجِدِ اَلسُّوقِ، تَعْدِلُ اِثْنَتَيْ عَشْرَةَ صَلاَةً وَ صَلاَةُ اَلرَّجُلِ فِي بَيْتِهِ تَعْدِلُ صَلاَةً وَاحِدَةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.702 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said :
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A prayer in Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) is equivalent to one thousand prayers. A prayer in the Great Mosque (Masjid al-Haram) is equivalent to one hundred thousand prayers. A prayer in the Mosque of the Tribe (Masjid al-Qabilah) is equivalent to twenty-five prayers. A prayer in the market mosque is equivalent to twelve prayers. A man's prayer in his home is equivalent to one prayer."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.703 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Whoever builds a mosque as small as a sandgrouse’s nest, Allah (swt) will build for them a house in Paradise."
records.arabicText: 703 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَنْ بَنَى مَسْجِداً كَمَفْحَصِ قَطَاةٍ بَنَى اَللَّهُ لَهُ بَيْتاً فِي اَلْجَنَّةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.703 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever builds a mosque as small as a sandgrouse’s nest, Allah (swt) will build for them a house in Paradise."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.704 - Abu Ubaidah Al-Hadha’ said:
"Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) passed by me while I was between Makkah and Madinah placing stones. I asked: 'Is this from that?'
Imam (as) said: 'Yes.'"
records.arabicText: 704 - وَقَالَ أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ اَلْحَذَّاءُ: وَ مَرَّ بِي أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ أَنَا بَيْنَ مَكَّةَ وَ اَلْمَدِينَةِ أَضَعُ اَلْأَحْجَارَ فَقُلْتُ هَذَا مِنْ ذَاكَ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.704 - Abu Ubaidah Al-Hadha’ said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) passed by me while I was between Makkah and Madinah placing stones. I asked: 'Is this from that?'
Imam (as) said: 'Yes.'"

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.705 - Ubaidullah ibn Ali Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about covered (roofed) mosques, whether standing (prayer) in them is disliked.
Imam (as) said: "Yes, but praying in them does not harm you."
records.arabicText: 705 - وَ سَأَلَ عُبَيْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْمَسَاجِدِ اَلْمُظَلَّلَةِ يُكْرَهُ اَلْقِيَامُ فِيهَا قَالَ «نَعَمْ وَ لَكِنْ لاَ تَضُرُّكُمُ اَلصَّلاَةُ فِيهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.705 - Ubaidullah ibn Ali Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about covered (roofed) mosques, whether standing (prayer) in them is disliked.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, but praying in them does not harm you."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.706 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"The first thing our Qa'im (the awaited savior Imam Muhammad ibn Hassan Al-Mahdi (as) ) will do is to break the roofs of the mosques and order them to be made as an open canopy (like the canopy of Musa (as))”.
records.arabicText: 706 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَوَّلُ مَا يَبْدَأُ بِهِ قَائِمُنَا سُقُوفُ اَلْمَسَاجِدِ فَيُكَسِّرُهَا وَ يَأْمُرُ بِهَا فَيُجْعَلُ عَرِيشاً كَعَرِيشِ مُوسَى.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.706 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The first thing our Qa'im (the awaited savior Imam Muhammad ibn Hassan Al-Mahdi (as) ) will do is to break the roofs of the mosques and order them to be made as an open canopy (like the canopy of Musa (as))”.

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.707 - When Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) saw the niches (mihrabs) in mosques, he would break them and say,
"It is as if they are the altars of the Jews."
records.arabicText: 707 - وَ كَانَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِذَا رَأَى اَلْمَحَارِيبَ فِي اَلْمَسَاجِدِ كَسَّرَهَا وَ يَقُولُ «كَأَنَّهَا مَذَابِحُ اَلْيَهُودِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.707 - When Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) saw the niches (mihrabs) in mosques, he would break them and say,
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is as if they are the altars of the Jews."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.708 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) saw a mosque in Kufa that had been decorated or elevated and said:
"It is as if it is a church. Mosques are not to be adorned; they should be built plain (simple)."
records.arabicText: 708 - وَ رَأَى عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَسْجِداً بِالْكُوفَةِ قَدْ شُرِّفَ قَالَ «كَأَنَّهُ بِيعَةٌ إِنَّ اَلْمَسَاجِدَ لاَ تُشَرَّفُ تُبْنَى جُمّاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.708 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) saw a mosque in Kufa that had been decorated or elevated and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is as if it is a church. Mosques are not to be adorned; they should be built plain (simple)."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.709 - Abu Al-Hasan al-Awwal, Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), was asked about using clay mixed with straw to plaster the mosque or the house where prayers are offered.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 709 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلطِّينِ فِيهِ اَلتِّبْنُ يُطَيَّنُ بِهِ اَلْمَسْجِدُ أَوِ اَلْبَيْتُ اَلَّذِي يُصَلَّى فِيهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.709 - Abu Al-Hasan al-Awwal, Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as), was asked about using clay mixed with straw to plaster the mosque or the house where prayers are offered.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.710 - He was asked about using plaster that had been prepared with dung for plastering a mosque.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm."
records.arabicText: 710 - وَ سُئِلَ: عَنْ بَيْتٍ قَدْ كَانَ اَلْجِصُّ يُطْبَخُ بِالْعَذِرَةِ أَ يَصْلُحُ أَنْ يُجَصَّصَ بِهِ اَلْمَسْجِدُ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ.

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.711 - Abu Al-Hasan al-Awwal, Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) was asked about a house that had been used as a latrine for a period of time and whether it would be suitable to make it into a mosque.
Imam (as) said: "If it is cleaned and repaired, then there is no harm."
records.arabicText: 711 - وَ سُئِلَ: عَنْ بَيْتٍ قَدْ كَانَ حَشّاً زَمَاناً هَلْ يَصْلُحُ أَنْ يُجْعَلَ مَسْجِداً فَقَالَ «إِذَا نُظِّفَ وَ أُصْلِحَ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.711 - Abu Al-Hasan al-Awwal, Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) was asked about a house that had been used as a latrine for a period of time and whether it would be suitable to make it into a mosque.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If it is cleaned and repaired, then there is no harm."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.712 - Ubaidullah ibn Ali Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a mosque within a house, where the owners might wish to expand part of it or move it from its original location.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that."
Ubaydullah then asked: "Would it be suitable for a place that was previously used as a latrine for a period of time to be cleaned and made into a mosque?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if soil is placed over it to cover it, as this cleanses and purifies it."
records.arabicText: 712 - وَ سَأَلَ عُبَيْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: فِي مَسْجِدٍ يَكُونُ فِي اَلدَّارِ فَيَبْدُو لِأَهْلِهِ أَنْ يَتَوَسَّعُوا بِطَائِفَةٍ مِنْهُ أَوْ يُحَوِّلُوهُ عَنْ مَكَانِهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ» قَالَ فَقُلْتُ فَيَصْلُحُ اَلْمَكَانُ اَلَّذِي كَانَ حَشّاً زَمَاناً أَنْ يُنَظَّفَ وَ يُتَّخَذَ مَسْجِداً، قَالَ «نَعَمْ إِذَا أُلْقِيَ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ اَلتُّرَابِ مَا يُوَارِيهِ فَإِنَّ ذَلِكَ يُنَظِّفُهُ وَ يُطَهِّرُهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.712 - Ubaidullah ibn Ali Al-Halabi asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a mosque within a house, where the owners might wish to expand part of it or move it from its original location.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no harm in that."
Ubaydullah then asked: "Would it be suitable for a place that was previously used as a latrine for a period of time to be cleaned and made into a mosque?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if soil is placed over it to cover it, as this cleanses and purifies it."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.713 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to say:
"Whoever frequently visits the mosques will attain one of eight benefits: a brother (friend) beneficial in the cause of Allah (swt), newly acquired knowledge, a definitive verse (of guidance), a mercy awaited, a word that turns him away from misguidance, hearing a word that leads him to guidance, abandoning a sin out of fear (of Allah (swt)) or out of shame”.
records.arabicText: 713 - وَ كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ: «مَنِ اِخْتَلَفَ إِلَى اَلْمَسَاجِدِ أَصَابَ إِحْدَى اَلثَّمَانِ أَخاً مُسْتَفَاداً فِي اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَوْ عِلْماً مُسْتَطْرَفاً أَوْ آيَةً مُحْكَمَةً أَوْ رَحْمَةً مُنْتَظَرَةً أَوْ كَلِمَةً تَرُدُّهُ عَنْ رَدًى أَوْ يَسْمَعُ كَلِمَةً تَدُلُّهُ عَلَى هُدًى أَوْ يَتْرُكُ ذَنْباً خَشْيَةً أَوْ حَيَاءً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.713 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) used to say:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever frequently visits the mosques will attain one of eight benefits: a brother (friend) beneficial in the cause of Allah (swt), newly acquired knowledge, a definitive verse (of guidance), a mercy awaited, a word that turns him away from misguidance, hearing a word that leads him to guidance, abandoning a sin out of fear (of Allah (swt)) or out of shame”.

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.714 - The Prophet (sw), heard a man searching for a lost item in the mosque and said:
"Say to him, 'May Allah (swt) not return your lost item to you,' for the mosque was not built for this purpose."
records.arabicText: 714 - وَ سَمِعَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ رَجُلاً يُنْشِدُ ضَالَّةً فِي اَلْمَسْجِدِ فَقَالَ «قُولُوا لَهُ لاَ رَدَّ اَللَّهُ عَلَيْكَ ضَالَّتَكَ فَإِنَّهَا لِغَيْرِ هَذَا بُنِيَتْ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.714 - The Prophet (sw), heard a man searching for a lost item in the mosque and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Say to him, 'May Allah (swt) not return your lost item to you,' for the mosque was not built for this purpose."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.715 - Imam (as) said: "Keep your children, the insane, raising of voices, buying and selling, seeking lost items, and administering legal punishments and rulings away from your mosques”.
records.arabicText: 715 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «جَنِّبُوا مَسَاجِدَكُمْ صِبْيَانَكُمْ وَ مَجَانِينَكُمْ وَ رَفْعَ أَصْوَاتِكُمْ وَ شِرَاءَكُمْ وَ بَيْعَكُمْ وَ اَلضَّالَّةَ وَ اَلْحُدُودَ وَ اَلْأَحْكَامَ.

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.717 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"If any of you takes a pebble from the mosque, let them return it to its place or to another mosque, for it glorifies Allah (swt)”.
records.arabicText: 717 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا أَخْرَجَ أَحَدُكُمُ اَلْحَصَاةَ مِنَ اَلْمَسْجِدِ فَلْيَرُدَّهَا فِي مَكَانِهَا أَوْ فِي مَسْجِدٍ آخَرَ فَإِنَّهَا تُسَبِّحُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.717 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If any of you takes a pebble from the mosque, let them return it to its place or to another mosque, for it glorifies Allah (swt)”.

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.718 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The best mosques for your women are their homes."
records.arabicText: 718 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «خَيْرُ مَسَاجِدِ نِسَائِكُمُ اَلْبُيُوتُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.718 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The best mosques for your women are their homes."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.719 - Imam (as) was asked about endowing property to mosques, and He said:
"It is not permissible, for the Magians endowed property for their fire temples”.
records.arabicText: 719 - وَ سُئِلَ: عَنِ اَلْوُقُوفِ عَلَى اَلْمَسَاجِدِ فَقَالَ «لاَ يَجُوزُ فَإِنَّ اَلْمَجُوسَ أَوْقَفُوا عَلَى بُيُوتِ اَلنَّارِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.719 - Imam (as) was asked about endowing property to mosques, and He said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is not permissible, for the Magians endowed property for their fire temples”.

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.720 - It is narrated "In the Torah, it is written that 'My houses on earth are the mosques. Glad tidings to the servant who purifies themselves in their home and then visits Me in My house. Surely, it is incumbent upon the host to honor their guest. Give glad tidings to those who walk in the darkness to the mosques of a radiant light on the Day of Judgment.'"
records.arabicText: 720 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ فِي اَلتَّوْرَاةِ مَكْتُوباً أَنَّ بُيُوتِي فِي اَلْأَرْضِ اَلْمَسَاجِدُ فَطُوبَى لِعَبْدٍ تَطَهَّرَ فِي بَيْتِهِ ثُمَّ زَارَنِي فِي بَيْتِي أَلاَ إِنَّ عَلَى اَلْمَزُورِ كَرَامَةَ اَلزَّائِرِ أَلاَ بَشِّرِ اَلْمَشَّاءِينَ فِي اَلظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَى اَلْمَسَاجِدِ بِالنُّورِ اَلسَّاطِعِ يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ.

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.721 - It is narrated: "The houses in which prayers are offered at night shine with light for the inhabitants of the heavens just as the stars shine with light for the inhabitants of the earth."
records.arabicText: 721 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ اَلْبُيُوتَ اَلَّتِي يُصَلَّى فِيهَا بِاللَّيْلِ يُضِيءُ نُورُهَا لِأَهْلِ اَلسَّمَاءِ كَمَا يُضِيءُ نُورُ اَلْكَوَاكِبِ لِأَهْلِ اَلْأَرْضِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.721 - It is narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The houses in which prayers are offered at night shine with light for the inhabitants of the heavens just as the stars shine with light for the inhabitants of the earth."

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.722 - It is narrated that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), passed by a tall minaret and ordered it to be demolished.
He then said: "The minaret should not be raised higher than the roof of the mosque”.
records.arabicText: 722 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَرَّ عَلَى مَنَارَةٍ طَوِيلَةٍ فَأَمَرَ بِهَدْمِهَا، ثُمَّ قَالَ «لاَ تُرْفَعُ اَلْمَنَارَةُ إِلاَّ مَعَ سَطْحِ اَلْمَسْجِدِ ».

records.chapter: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.723 - "And indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, intends to bring punishment upon all the people of the earth without exception. But when He looks at the elderly, who move their feet to the prayers and the children learning the Qur'an, He has mercy on them and withholds that punishment”.
records.arabicText:
723 - «وَ إِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى لَيُرِيدُ عَذَابَ أَهْلِ اَلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً حَتَّى لاَ يُحَاشِيَ مِنْهُمْ أَحَداً فَإِذَا نَظَرَ إِلَى اَلشِّيبِ نَاقِلِي أَقْدَامِهِمْ إِلَى اَلصَّلَوَاتِ وَ اَلْوِلْدَانِ يَتَعَلَّمُونَ اَلْقُرْآنَ رَحِمَهُمُ اَللَّهُ فَأَخَّرَ ذَلِكَ عَنْهُمْ.
ومن أراد دخول المسجد فليدخل على سكون ووقار فإن المساجد بيوت الله وأحب البقاع إليه، وأحبهم إلى الله عز وجل [رجلا] أولهم دخولا وآخرهم خروجا.

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.724 - The Prophet, said:
"I was given five things that no one was given before me: The entire earth was made a place of prayer and a means of purification for me, I was aided with fear (cast into the hearts of my enemies), war booty was made lawful for me, I was given concise speech (Jawaami' al-Kalim), and I was granted intercession."
records.arabicText: 724 - قَالَ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «أُعْطِيتُ خَمْساً لَمْ يُعْطَهَا أَحَدٌ قَبْلِي جُعِلَتْ لِيَ اَلْأَرْضُ مَسْجِداً وَ طَهُوراً وَ نُصِرْتُ بِالرُّعْبِ وَ أُحِلَّ لِيَ اَلْمَغْنَمُ وَ أُعْطِيتُ جَوَامِعَ اَلْكَلِمِ وَ أُعْطِيتُ اَلشَّفَاعَةَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.724 - The Prophet, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I was given five things that no one was given before me: The entire earth was made a place of prayer and a means of purification for me, I was aided with fear (cast into the hearts of my enemies), war booty was made lawful for me, I was given concise speech (Jawaami' al-Kalim), and I was granted intercession."

records.chapter: Places Where Prayer is Permissable and Where it is Not Permissable
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.725 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"There are ten places where prayer should not be performed: mud, water, baths, graves, paths frequently used by travelers, ant hills, camel resting places, watercourses, salty land (sabkha), and snow”.
records.arabicText: 725 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «عَشَرَةُ مَوَاضِعَ لاَ يُصَلَّى فِيهَا اَلطِّينُ وَ اَلْمَاءُ وَ اَلْحَمَّامُ وَ اَلْقُبُورُ وَ مَسَانُّ اَلطَّرِيقِ وَ قُرَى اَلنَّمْلِ وَ مَعَاطِنُ اَلْإِبِلِ وَ مَجْرَى اَلْمَاءِ، وَ اَلسَّبَخَةُ وَ اَلثَّلْجُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.725 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There are ten places where prayer should not be performed: mud, water, baths, graves, paths frequently used by travelers, ant hills, camel resting places, watercourses, salty land (sabkha), and snow”.
